steps.title: Identify what needs a linking verb
steps.content: The blank requires a form of “to be” to connect the subject “only Algerian authorities” to the state “permitted to make arrests.”

steps.title: Select the correct tense form
steps.content:
The passage uses present tense throughout: “Interpol can help“ with investigations, and the explanation describes current rules about how authority works in Algiers. “Are” (choice D) correctly uses present tense to express the current rule. Past tense forms (were, were being, had been) would wrongly suggest the authorization was only true in the past.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
